detail:
Confirmed real regression introduced by the FA-0913 audit fix. Commit c1124d2 added `raw_user_text = redact_user_message(raw_user_text, tenant)` at poller.py:1375, and that now-redacted text is passed as user_text_excerpt (poller.py:1435) and stored verbatim on PendingMessage.user_text (pending_queue.py:375). The dropped-message apology _send_apology_for_dropped_pending_message (pending_queue.py:1106-1159) reads msg.user_text, runs only strip_internal_framing — whose _INTERNAL_FRAMING_TAGS (error_messages.py:959-965) covers System/Now/chat/User tapped button/Photo attached but NOT [PERSON_N]/[EMAIL_ADDRESS_N] placeholders — and sends it to the user with no rehydrate_text call (Telegram httpx.post 1150-1153; LINE _send_line_text 1129). Every other outbound channel path DOES rehydrate (poller.py:331, pending_queue.py:1521/1568, cron_delivery.py:175, chat_views.py:435, siri_views.py:108); only these apology paths were missed. The new test tests_audit_C01.py:74-75 codifies the redacted excerpt at rest. Pre-fix the excerpt was the user's real text, so the apology quoted real words — a true behavioural regression. The claim is slightly understated: a SECOND identical leak path exists, _send_apology_for_stale_pending_message (pending_queue.py:1028), which reads msg.user_text as an excerpt with neither strip_internal_framing NOR rehydrate. User impact is real but bounded: the apology goes to the subscriber THEMSELVES (the owner of the PII), so there is no third-party privacy exposure — it is a cosmetic/UX defect where a user whose dropped/stale message contained a name/email/phone sees e.g. 'It started with: \"[EMAIL_ADDRESS_1] about it…\"' instead of their own words. It only fires on a rare error path (PII in the message AND a 3-attempt delivery failure or a too-stale queue). Hence low severity, not medium.</final_detail>
<suggested_fix>In both apology helpers, rehydrate the stored excerpt before quoting. At pending_queue.py:1112 wrap with rehydrate_for_tenant: `excerpt = strip_internal_framing(rehydrate_for_tenant(tenant, msg.user_text or '')).strip().replace('\n', ' ')`, and at pending_queue.py:1028 apply both rehydrate_for_tenant and strip_internal_framing similarly (the stale path is currently missing the framing strip too). Import `from apps.pii.redactor import rehydrate_for_tenant`. Update tests_audit_C01.py to assert the at-rest stored excerpt is redacted (current behaviour, correct for the no-PII-to-LLM goal) AND add a test that the apology TEXT the user receives is rehydrated back to real words.</suggested_fix>
<fix_complexity>trivial</fix_complexity>
<evidence>apps/router/poller.py:1375 (raw_user_text = redact_user_message(raw_user_text, tenant) — added in commit c1124d2)</evidence>
<evidence>apps/router/poller.py:1435 (user_text_excerpt=raw_user_text)</evidence>
<evidence>apps/router/pending_queue.py:375 (user_text=user_text_excerpt stored on row)</evidence>
<evidence>apps/router/pending_queue.py:1112 + 1150-1153 (dropped apology: strip_internal_framing only, httpx.post with no rehydrate)</evidence>
<evidence>apps/router/pending_queue.py:1028 + 1043 (stale apology: SECOND leak — no strip_internal_framing, no rehydrate)</evidence>
<evidence>apps/router/error_messages.py:959-965 (_INTERNAL_FRAMING_TAGS excludes PERSON/EMAIL placeholders)</evidence>
<evidence>apps/pii/redactor.py:198 rehydrate_text / 230 rehydrate_for_tenant exist; used by all other outbound paths (poller.py:331, pending_queue.py:1521/1568, cron_delivery.py:175) but not the apology paths</evidence>
<evidence>apps/router/tests_audit_C01.py:74-75 asserts placeholder IS in user_text_excerpt (codifies at-rest redaction)</evidence>
</invoke>
evidence: apps/router/pending_queue.py:1112 excerpt=strip_internal_framing(msg.user_text); 1150-1153 httpx.post apology with no rehydrate
apps/router/error_messages.py:959-969 _INTERNAL_FRAMING_TAGS does not include PII placeholders
apps/router/tests_audit_C01.py:71-73 asserts placeholder IS in user_text_excerpt
